data.body:
G3 child-coverage audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 2359 "Surface outbound sub-op telemetry + silent-no-op canary"), 4-bucket per parent criterion, cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/822a/8937/fe57). VERDICT: clean pairing — no G3 finding; no uncovered parent criterion remains across the roster.

OWNED — FULLY covered by THIS child:
- Parent AC "Outbound sub-op telemetry (links_applied/comments_applied/labels_applied) is surfaced in the structured batch outcome" → 2359 AC1 (structured outbound batch outcome + RECON: line surface all three, parity with inbound applier). FULLY. (labels_applied is explicitly named in 2359 AC1, so this clause is not a coverage gap; the labels list-vs-count representation question is a G4 consistency matter, tracked separately on this ticket, not a G3 coverage gap.)
- Parent AC "A `computed > 0 && applied == 0` sub-op condition fails loudly, after the warn-first rollout in Rollout & rollback" → 2359 AC2 (test asserts computed>0 && applied==0 fails, reproducing the link drop) + 2359 AC3 (fail-loud invariant rolls out warn-first behind a flag; promotion to hard-fail and reversion to warn = flag flip, no other code change — exact match to the parent Rollout & rollback clause). FULLY.
- Parent SC "A silent sub-op no-op (e.g. links computed but never applied) fails a test/canary instead of passing green" → 2359 AC2. FULLY.

CONTRIBUTED: parent SC "New tests assert behavior/contract … zero change-detector tests added" via 2359 AC5 (canary asserts the count invariant, not specific log strings); parent AC "all six child stories closed; full reconciler suite green" via 2359 AC4 (sync semantics otherwise unchanged, suite green).

DELEGATION — parent criteria this child does NOT own are correctly assigned to named siblings, consistent with this child's declared OUT items (probe wiring → story F/822a; applier consolidation → story D/33d0):
- SSOT snapshot schema + fetcher-validated-vs-schema + each-differ-reads-validated + one round-trip contract through the production path with real fixtures + SC "producer/consumers cannot drift on shape" → 8937 (+ fe57 fixtures/fake, 59ef verified-fake honesty).
- Collapse-to-single-production-path + retired-path-deleted + SC "outbound exercised through the production code path, not a parallel dead path" → 33d0.
- Bidirectional comment/link/parent round-trip asserted hermetically + every expected-bug probe guard de-encoded + SC "bidirectional round-trips asserted; no probe encodes a bug as expected" → 822a.

ANTI-FP — parent AC "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail for reconciler tests is committed and referenced by new tests (owned by story B)" is FULLY covered, NOT an absence: the AC verbatim names its owner (story B = sibling 8937), and 8937 owns it (8937 description "Owns the epic's guardrail artifact …" + 8937 AC5 "A documented anti-change-detector guardrail (ADR-style note …) is committed and referenced by the new tests"). Under the G3 ANTI-FP rule (a criterion covered-by-definition by a NAMED consumer counts), this is FULLY covered. The earlier recurring "AC6 governs-new-tests PARTIALLY covered" finding on this ticket was raised against paraphrased wording that does not match the plan text and is withdrawn; not re-filed.

4-bucket summary for this pairing: FULLY (owned by THIS child) — parent "sub-op telemetry surfaced", parent "computed>0 && applied==0 fails loud (warn-first)", parent SC "silent no-op fails canary"; CONTRIBUTED — parent SC "zero change-detector" + parent AC "suite green"; COVERED-BY-NAMED-SIBLING — schema/contract/round-trip (8937/fe57/59ef), applier collapse + production-path (33d0), bidirectional round-trip + probe de-encoding (822a), documented guardrail (8937 = story B, named owner); STRUCTURAL/AGGREGATE — parent "all six closed; suite green". UNCOVERED — none.

No G3 coverage finding for this pairing. The union of children covers every parent acceptance/success criterion, and THIS child fully delivers its owned share (outbound sub-op telemetry + the warn-first silent-no-op canary).
